summary: Crystal‑clear second set with haunting Black Peter & soaring Dupree's
blurb: The Milwaukee show delivers a high‑energy first set highlighted by Deal, Row Jimmy and Samson & Delilah. The second set shines with Black Peter, Dupree's Diamond Blues and an inspired NFA jam. Betty Cantor‑Jackson’s soundboard capture makes this one of the cleanest recordings of 1978.
review:
On February 4, 1978 at the Milwaukee Auditorium the Dead were firing on all cylinders. The first set rolled out classic rockers – Jack Straw, Tennessee Jed, a seamless run through Deal and Row Jimmy that kept the crowd moving. Phil’s bass locked in tight, while Jerry’s storytelling guitar tone added depth to every lyric. Donna Jean’s vocal power rose especially on Dupree's Diamond Blues, and Keith’s organ swelled under the band’s dynamic shifts.
The second set is where the night truly sparkles: a haunting Black Peter segues into an unrelenting Not Fade Away/NFA jam before bursting into a soaring Around & Around. The drum break leading into the NFA showcases Mickey and Bill at their most kinetic, with Bill’s high‑hat shimmer captured in crystalline detail on both the SBD and matrix sources. Betty Cantor‑Jackson’s soundboard tape is unsurpassed – crystal‑clear, crisp, and free of any audible flaws, making this performance a must‑listen for anyone seeking a pristine 1978 snapshot.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: All three recordings are marked as positive, repeatedly praising the band's tight playing, standout songs (Black Peter, Dupree's Diamond Blues, Deal) and flawless audio capture. No negative comments about performance appear, so a strong positive sentiment adjustment is applied.

best_recording.reason: Betty Cantor‑Jackson’s soundboard provides crystal‑clear, unsurpassed audio that captures the full energy of the second set and highlights the band’s tight playing.

key_highlights: Second set shines with haunting Black Peter and soaring Dupree's Diamond Blues.
First set delivers high‑energy rockers like Deal, Row Jimmy and Samson & Delilah.
Crystal‑clear Betty Cantor‑Jackson SBD captures Bill’s shimmering hi‑hat and tight drum break.
Audience tape still offers solid listening with great vocal moments from Donna Jean.

band_performance.Jerry: Jerry tells his stories with perfect tone, delivering inspired solos on Deal and Dupree's while keeping the narrative thread alive throughout the show.
band_performance.Phil: Phil’s bass is strong and punchy, anchoring the high‑energy set and cutting through the mix when mids are boosted.
band_performance.Brent: Keith Godchaux provides lush, clear organ work that shines on Dupree's Diamond Blues and the second‑set jams; Donna Jean adds soaring vocal highlights.
band_performance.Mickey: Mickey Hart locks in with powerful fills, driving the NFA jam after the drum break and keeping the groove relentless.
band_performance.Billy: Bill Kreutzmann’s crisp high‑hat shimmer and tight drumming are captured beautifully on the matrix recording and SBD, adding sparkle to the rhythm section.
